'''Harry Smith''' (born 25 January 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|so}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and both {{rlnt|England}} and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and the [[London Skolars]] in [[RFL League 1]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire, England]].

==Career==
[[File:Harry Smith Wigan.jpg|thumb|180px|right|Smith playing for Wigan in 2018]]
Smith spent the majority of the 2019 on loan to [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Swinton Lions]], and [[RFL League 1|League 1]] side [[London Skolars]]. He made his début for [[Wigan Warriors]] in September 2019 against the [[Catalans Dragons]], scoring his first try for the club in a 46–12 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921828|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v Catalans Dragons|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

Smith scored his first try of the 2020 season in the later expunged victory over [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11933307/wigan-warriors-32-10-toronto-wolfpack-bevan-french-and-liam-farrell-keep-wolfpack-at-bay|title=Wigan Warriors 32-10 Toronto Wolfpack: Bevan French and Liam Farrell keep Wolfpack at bay|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> During the 2020 season, Smith became a regular squad player and won his first piece of silverware with the club – the [[League Leaders' Shield]].

The 2021 season saw Smith take over goal kicking duties from [[Zak Hardaker]] as he became a regular starter for Wigan. However, mixed accuracy saw Hardaker reclaim kicking duties towards the end of the season.

In March 2022, Smith kicked the winning drop goals in their victories over [[Toulouse Olympique]], winning 29–28,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/toulouse-vs-wigan/report/56255/|title=Toulouse Olympique 28-29 Wigan Warriors: Harry Smith drop goal keeps visitors unbeaten in Super League|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref> and [[Hull F.C.]], winning 19–18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/640/cpsprodpb/16A18/production/_123969629_shutterstock_editorial_12876644z.jpg/|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 19-18 Hull FC - Late Harry Smith drop-goal gives hosts win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> Smith reestablish himself as the club's primary goal kicker in April, in July he scored a try and kicked eight goals in Wigan's 60–0 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live-wigan-warriors-v-hull-7340022/|title=LIVE Wigan Warriors v Hull FC live updates and match action|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=16 July 2022|archive-date=17 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220717231055/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live-wigan-warriors-v-hull-7340022|url-status=dead}}</ref>

The [[2022 Challenge Cup]] campaign saw Smith awarded man of the match in the semi-final in Wigan's 20–18 [[St Helens R.F.C.–Wigan Warriors rivalry|derby]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Elland Road]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/harry-smith-wigan-warriors-cup-23909304/|title=Harry Smith lays down challenge to Wigan Warriors teammates after semi-final win|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=17 May 2022|archive-date=10 May 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220510104356/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/harry-smith-wigan-warriors-cup-23909304|url-status=dead}}</ref> In the [[2022 Challenge Cup Final|final]], Smith scored a first half try and set up the match winning try towards the end of the match which saw a 16–14 victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and Wigan lift their first Challenge Cup since 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>

In round 22 of the 2023 Super League season, Smith kicked the winning drop goal for Wigan in golden point extra-time as they won the match 13–12 over Hull F.C.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-harry-smith-reflects-on-his-winning-golden-point-drop-goal-against-hull-fc-4261526/|title=Wigan Warriors: Harry Smith reflects on his winning golden point drop-goal against Hull FC|website=www.wigantoday.net |date=19 August 2023 }}</ref> Wigan finished the regular season winning the [[League Leaders' Shield]].

In the playoff semi-final, Smith scored 7/7 conversions in Wigan's 42–12 over [[Hull KR]],<ref>{{Cite web | title=Match Report - Wigan 42 - 12 Hull K R {{!}} 07 Oct 2023 | url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-hull-k-r/report/58418 | access-date=2024-12-26 | website=www.skysports.com}}</ref> and in the  [[2023 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] scored a conversion and two penalties in the 10–2 victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]] seeing Wigan win the 2023 Super League.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
Following the conclusion of the 2023 season, Smith was nominated for the [[IRL Golden Boot Award]]. On 24 February 2024, Smith played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|work=BBC Sport |date=24 February 2024 }}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Smith played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=8 June 2024 }}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Smith played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Calvert |first1=Lee |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com |date=12 October 2024 }}</ref>

Smith received praise for his performance in Wigan's 2025 away fixture against Hull KR.<ref>{{cite web | last1=Bower | first1=Aaron | title=Wigan end Hull KR's unbeaten run after Harry Smith inspires devastating finale | work=The Guardian | date=11 April 2025 | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2025/apr/11/hullkr-wigan-super-league-match-report }}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Smith played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Smith played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2019 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

Smith made his full {{rlnt|England}} début on 29 April 2023 in the 64–0 victory over {{rlnt|France}} at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]]. Smith scored one try and kicked nine goals in the match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/65418274|title=England beat France 64-0 - hat-tricks for Handley and Williams|work=BBC Sport |date=28 April 2023 }}</ref>
In October and November 2023, Smith played in [[2023 Tonga rugby league tour of England|England's three match test series against Tonga]] in which England won 3–0.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2023/nov/06/england-tonga-series-world-cup-huddersfield-st-helens-leeds/|title=England's 3-0 win over Tonga shows why they are the third best team|newspaper=The Guardian|date=6 November 2023 |last1=Willacy |first1=Gavin }}</ref> Smith was awarded player of the series, being praised for his kicking game and playmaking, kicking 22 points for England.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/shaun-wanes-tinkering-pays-off-player-of-series-harry-smith-bumper-headingley-crowd-five-takeaways-as-england-seal-test-series-whitewash-over-tonga | title=Shaun Wane's tinkering pays off, Player of Series Harry Smith, Bumper Headingley crowd - Five takeaways as England seal test series whitewash over Tonga | date=4 November 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web | title=England claim series whitewash over disappointing Tonga as Harry Smith proves international worth | date=4 November 2023 | url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/england-claim-series-whitewash-over-disappointing-tonga-as-harry-smith-proves-international-worth-4397891 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231105041851/https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/england-claim-series-whitewash-over-disappointing-tonga-as-harry-smith-proves-international-worth-4397891 | access-date=2024-12-26 | archive-date=2023-11-05}}</ref>
On June 29, 2024, Smith kicked 4 goals from 8 attempts as England beat France 40- 8, in France

He played in England's first test against Samoa, scoring 5 goals from 6 attempts, in a 34- 12 England victory. He kicked four conversions and a penalty in the 2nd test 34–16 win over {{rlnt|Samoa}} on 2 Nov 2024 at Headingley.
